message:
fix(time): detach fallback timer threads instead of joining on drop

Sleep's fallback timer threads (used when no async timer driver is
available) were previously joined on Drop/reset, which blocks the
executor thread. This is problematic when Sleep is dropped from an async
context — joining blocks the entire runtime until the timer thread
exits.

Replace handle.join() with drop(handle) in all four sites (reset,
reset_at, Future::poll, Drop) to intentionally detach the threads.
OS threads naturally clean themselves up upon exit, so no resource
leak occurs.

Updated comments to reflect the intentional detach semantics and
explain why joining was incorrect for an async runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
